key_findings: Three independent scores designed: KRIL (0-100, asset strategic value), Asset Risk Score (0-100, vulnerability severity density), Bounty Potential Score (0-100, submission ROI) — single composite score rejected as insufficient for multi-use-case needs

KRIL is forward-looking (how worth scanning is this asset?) — Risk Score is backward-looking (how dangerous is this asset confirmed to be?) — Bounty Potential is ROI-focused (which finding to submit?) — distinct queries require distinct scores
All three scores are explainability-first — signal component values stored on Neo4j Subdomain node alongside final score; analyst can inspect why a score is high without re-running scorer

All weights externalized to score_config.json (hot-reloadable); ML model rejected in favor of weighted sum — ML requires labeled training data, is not explainable without SHAP, cannot be hot-adjusted without retraining
Scoring pipeline is async/pipelined (not inline) — scoring of batch N runs concurrently with scanning of batch N+1; achieves ≤ 10% effective overhead vs 77% overhead if inline
KRIL drives AWSEM queue reordering: HTTP queue ordered by KRIL desc after each DNS batch; Enrich queue ordered by bounty_potential desc after each HTTP batch
Top-decile precision validation: ≥ 35% of all findings expected in top 10% of assets by KRIL score → ≥ 25% precision uplift vs naive ordering
Enrich KRIL threshold (default: 50) gates which targets receive nuclei/katana enrichment — low-KRIL targets skip expensive enrichment; threshold configurable in score_config.json
Bounty potential score is per-finding (not per-asset) — analyst needs to know which specific finding to submit; asset-level aggregation (max across findings) is secondary convenience
score_config.json must be versioned; config_version stored alongside each computed score for auditability — weight changes produce score discontinuities that need traceable provenance

decisions.title: Three independent scores (KRIL, risk, bounty)
decisions.summary: Three separate scores for distinct use cases: queue prioritization (KRIL), remediation priority (risk), bug bounty submission (bounty potential)
decisions.rationale: A single composite score cannot be tuned for different use cases simultaneously. KRIL prioritizes scanning resources; risk prioritizes remediation; bounty potential prioritizes submission. Separate scores are independently queryable and independently configurable.

decisions.title: Weighted sum with externalized config over ML model
decisions.summary: All scores computed as weighted sums of structured signals; weights in score_config.json; hot-reloadable without controller restart
decisions.rationale: ML models require labeled training data (which findings led to accepted bounties), are opaque without SHAP wrappers, and cannot be hot-adjusted without retraining. Weighted sums are fully explainable, immediately deployable, and threshold-adjustable in seconds.

decisions.title: Pipelined async scoring (not inline)
decisions.summary: Scoring of batch N runs asynchronously concurrent with scanning of batch N+1; effective overhead ≤ 10% vs 77% if inline
decisions.rationale: Inline scoring blocks the scan hot path. At 700k DNS/hr, batch = 52s. KRIL computation for 10k targets at 50/T2-call × 200ms = 40s — 77% overhead inline. Async pipeline overlaps scoring and scanning, reducing effective overhead to ≤ 10%.

decisions.title: Explainability fields stored on Neo4j node
decisions.summary: Signal component values stored as kril_signals{}, risk_signals{}, bounty_signals{} alongside score on Subdomain node
decisions.rationale: Analysts seeing KRIL = 74 need to understand why. Explainability stored on node makes reasoning inspectable in Asset Detail view without re-running scorer. Required for analyst trust in automated prioritization.

decisions.title: Bounty potential computed per-finding, aggregated per-asset
decisions.summary: bounty_potential_score computed for each Finding node; Subdomain node stores MAX across its findings as subdomain-level bounty score
decisions.rationale: Bug bounty submissions are per-finding. Analyst needs to know which specific finding to submit, not just which asset. Per-finding score preserves this resolution; asset-level max is a navigation convenience.

asset_risk_score.computation_trigger: After each Enrich batch completes for target

bounty_potential_score.granularity: per-finding; Subdomain stores MAX across its findings

signals.name: uniqueness_factor
signals.formula: 1.0 if first occurrence of template on target else 0.5

bounty_potential_score.final_formula: round((sum of signal × weight) × 100, 1) per finding; Subdomain.bounty_potential_score = MAX across findings
bounty_potential_score.write_back_target: Finding node property bounty_potential_score + Subdomain node property bounty_potential_score

http_queue.reordering_trigger: After each KRIL computation (post DNS batch)

enrich_queue.reordering_trigger: After each bounty potential computation (post HTTP batch)

scoring_pipeline.description: Scoring of batch N runs concurrently with scanning of batch N+1

top_decile_validation.method: After first full scan cycle, rank all subdomains by KRIL score; count findings in top 10% vs bottom 90%
top_decile_validation.validation_frequency: After each full DNS+HTTP+Enrich cycle completion

risk_model.title: KRIL stale under rapid scan expansion
risk_model.mitigation: Recency_bonus decays KRIL for unscanned assets; ORS alert on kril_age > 24h

risk_model.title: Top-decile precision below 25% uplift
risk_model.mitigation: Validate after first full cycle; adjust signal weights in score_config.json

risk_model.title: Scoring pipeline latency exceeds 10% overhead
risk_model.mitigation: Pipeline scoring is async; overlap with next batch; monitor kril_computation_latency_sec as ORS signal

risk_model.title: score_config changes produce score discontinuity without provenance
risk_model.mitigation: Version score_config.json; store config_version alongside each computed score

recommended_actions.action: Externalize all weights to score_config.json; hot-reloadable without controller restart
Store explainability JSON with every score in Neo4j — analyst-inspectable reasoning
Run pipelined async scoring (batch N scoring concurrent with batch N+1 scanning)
Validate top-decile precision after first full scan cycle; target >= 35% of findings in top 10%
Version score_config.json; store config_version with each computed score
Monitor kril_computation_latency_sec as ORS signal; alert if > batch_cycle_time
Use KRIL score as Enrich queue primary sort; bounty_potential as secondary sort
Set enrich_kril_threshold = 50 (configurable); only enrich targets above threshold

assumptions: A1: KRIL computation runs as T2 batch (50 targets per call); total time proportional to batch count
A2: Neo4j supports arbitrary JSON property storage for explainability fields on Subdomain nodes
A3: score_config.json hot-reload does not require controller process restart
A4: Bounty program config (scope rules, reward tiers) optional; model produces proxy signals without it
A5: Top-decile precision uplift measured after first complete DNS+HTTP+Enrich cycle, not on partial data
A6: Info-severity findings excluded from risk score computation (info does not contribute to risk)
